operating_state: Revenue increased 45% to $65.2 billion driven by cardiometabolic health products, with Mounjaro and Zepbound generating combined revenues representing 56% of total company sales. Oncology and immunology segments contributed strong performance led by Verzenio and Taltz respectively. International markets showed broadly positive momentum with double-digit growth across Europe and Japan, though CVS Caremark's removal of Zepbound from preferred formularies negatively impacted U.S. patient access.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved to 83.0% from 81.3% driven by favorable product mix toward higher-margin incretin medicines and improved production costs, partially offset by lower realized prices. Research and development expenses increased 21% including acquired IPR&D charges for strategic acquisitions. Marketing and administrative expenses rose 29% with advertising costs doubling to support cardiometabolic product launches and capacity expansion investments across global manufacturing facilities.
material_events: Completed acquisitions of Scorpion Therapeutics, SiteOne Therapeutics, and Verve Therapeutics for combined charges and cash payments. Announced preliminary voluntary agreements with U.S. government including Medicare obesity medicine access by July 2026 and participation in direct-to-patient purchasing platform. HHS selected Trulicity and Verzenio for government-set pricing effective 2028. Repurchased shares and increased quarterly dividend to $1.73 per share.
macro_exposures: Inflation Reduction Act implementation accelerated with additional product selections for government price-setting creating revenue erosion risk ahead of patent expiries. Foreign currency translation generated positive impact from euro and yen strengthening. Cash tax payments increased driven by Ireland payments from higher production activity. New U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods with retaliatory measures create supply chain disruption risks while BIOSECURE Act legislation threatens Chinese supplier reliance.
forward_outlook: Management expects additional products selected for government-set Medicare pricing in future years accelerating revenue erosion for affected medicines. Orforglipron regulatory submissions progressing across major markets with pre-launch inventory capitalized. Manufacturing expansion initiatives committed with potential supply agreement payments if purchase requirements not met. Strategic focus on direct-to-patient channels through LillyDirect expansion to mitigate formulary restrictions.
uncertainty_profile: Definitive government agreement negotiations ongoing with implementation timelines and pricing terms unresolved carrying risks of delays and unfavorable outcomes. Multiple ongoing litigation includes insulin pricing, 340B program disputes, and incretin medicine product liability cases. Patent litigation risks persist across major products with generic and biosimilar challenges expected. Cybersecurity vulnerabilities from expanded third-party relationships and AI technology deployment create operational risks requiring ongoing protective investment.
